sections.title: Preamble
sections.content: We, the sovereign Filipino people, imploring the aid of Almighty God, in order to build a just and humane society and establish a Government that shall embody our ideals and aspirations, promote the common good, conserve and develop our patrimony, and secure to ourselves and our posterity the blessings of independence and democracy under the rule of law and a regime of truth, justice, freedom, love, equality, and peace, do ordain and promulgate this Constitution. Ratified: February 2, 1987.

sections.title: Key Articles Overview
rows: Philippine archipelago + all territories with sovereignty; terrestrial, fluvial, and aerial domains; territorial sea, seabed, subsoil, insular shelves.
Declaration of Principles and State Policies
Democratic and republican State; sovereignty in the people; renounces war; civilian authority supreme over military; separation of Church and State.
Due process; equal protection; freedom of speech, religion, press; right to information; rights of the accused; writ of habeas corpus.
Natural-born citizens; jus sanguinis principle; naturalization.
Voting age: at least 18; resident for at least 1 year; in voting place for at least 6 months before election.
Senate (24 senators, 6-year term) + House of Representatives (max 250 members, 3-year term).
President (40 years old, 6-year term, no reelection); Vice-President (6-year term, max 2 consecutive terms).
Supreme Court: 1 Chief Justice + 14 Associate Justices.
CSC, COMELEC, COA — 7-year term without reappointment.
Provinces, cities, municipalities, barangays; autonomous regions in Muslim Mindanao and Cordilleras.
'Public office is a public trust.' Impeachable officials; Ombudsman; Sandiganbayan.
Natural resources owned by State; 60% Filipino capital for public utilities; 70% for advertising.
Labor rights; agrarian reform; Commission on Human Rights.
Free public elementary and high school; Filipino and English as official languages.
Marriage is an inviolable social institution.
Philippine flag: red, white, blue with sun and three stars.
3/4 Congress vote, constitutional convention, or people's initiative (12% of registered voters).

sections.title: Bill of Rights — Key Sections
rows: Due process; equal protection of laws
Right against unreasonable searches and seizures
Privacy of communication; illegally obtained evidence is inadmissible
Freedom of speech, expression, press, peaceful assembly, and petition
Freedom of religion; no religious test for civil/political rights
Right to information on matters of public concern
Rights of the accused under custodial investigation (right to remain silent, right to counsel, no torture)
Right to bail; no excessive bail
Presumption of innocence; right to speedy/impartial/public trial
Privilege of writ of habeas corpus (suspended only for invasion or rebellion)
No excessive fines; no cruel/degrading punishment
No imprisonment for debt or poll tax
No ex post facto law or bill of attainder

sections.title: Executive Department
items: President: Natural-born citizen; at least 40 years old; 10-year residency; 6-year term; no reelection.
Vice-President: Same qualifications as President; 6-year term; not more than 2 consecutive terms.
Succession: Vice-President → Senate President → Speaker of the House.
Martial law: maximum 60 days; Congress can revoke.
Bills: President has 30 days to veto; Congress can override with 2/3 vote of all members of each house.
Appropriation, revenue, tariff bills must originate in the House of Representatives.

sections.title: Constitutional Commissions
rows: Natural-born; at least 35; proven capacity for public administration
Natural-born; at least 35; college degree; majority must be Bar members with 10+ years practice
Natural-born; at least 35; CPA with 10+ years auditing OR Bar member with 10+ years practice

sections.title: Impeachment
items: Impeachable officials: President, Vice-President, Members of the Supreme Court, Members of Constitutional Commissions, Ombudsman.
Grounds: culpable violation of the Constitution, treason, bribery, graft and corruption, other high crimes, betrayal of public trust.
The House initiates impeachment; the Senate tries and decides.
Conviction requires 2/3 vote of all Senate members.
No impeachment proceedings against the same official more than once within a year.
Ombudsman (Tanodbayan) — protector of the people; investigates public officials; 7-year term without reappointment.
